description: Add OpenRouter and Llama.cpp as first-class providers with full feature parity, agentic tool-execution loop, and cost tracking

userStories.title: Provider enum + type expansion
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the provider type system to accept 'openrouter' and 'llamacpp' so that workflows can target new providers.
acceptanceCriteria: z.enum in `packages/workflows/src/schemas/workflow.ts:32` includes 'openrouter' and 'llamacpp'
z.enum in `packages/workflows/src/schemas/dag-node.ts:120` includes 'openrouter' and 'llamacpp'
`AssistantClientFactory` type in `packages/workflows/src/deps.ts:229` accepts all 4 providers
`WorkflowConfig.assistant` in `deps.ts:241` accepts all 4 providers
`GlobalConfig.defaultAssistant` in `config-types.ts:77` accepts all 4 providers
`RepoConfig.assistant` in `config-types.ts:139` accepts all 4 providers
`MergedConfig.assistant` in `config-types.ts:241` accepts all 4 providers
`SafeConfig.assistant` in `config-types.ts:301` accepts all 4 providers
New `OpenRouterAssistantDefaults` interface with `model?`, `apiKey?`, `siteUrl?`, `siteName?`
New `LlamaCppAssistantDefaults` interface with `model?`, `endpoint?`
`GlobalConfig.assistants`, `RepoConfig.assistants`, `MergedConfig.assistants`, `WorkflowConfig.assistants` include `openrouter?` and `llamacpp?` keys
`isModelCompatible()` in `model-validation.ts` handles openrouter (accept `vendor/model` format) and llamacpp (accept any string)
Claude aliases ('sonnet', 'opus', 'haiku') rejected for non-Claude providers
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Touch points: `packages/workflows/src/schemas/workflow.ts:32`, `packages/workflows/src/schemas/dag-node.ts:120`, `packages/workflows/src/deps.ts:229,241`, `packages/workflows/src/model-validation.ts:1-16`, `packages/core/src/config/config-types.ts:77,139,241,301`. Export a `ProviderType` union from a shared location to avoid repeating the literal union. The `config-loader.ts:223` env override guard also needs updating.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 1. Files: provider.ts, workflow.ts, dag-node.ts, deps.ts, config-types.ts, config-loader.ts, model-validation.ts, dag-executor.ts, executor.ts, config.schemas.ts, types/index.ts, schemas/index.ts, model-validation.test.ts, config-loader.test.ts

userStories.title: Config loader defaults for new providers
userStories.description: As an operator, I want config loading to handle openrouter and llamacpp assistant defaults so that new providers are configured via `.archon/config.yaml`.
acceptanceCriteria: `config-loader.ts` merges `assistants.openrouter` and `assistants.llamacpp` sections from config YAML
Default values: `openrouter: {}`, `llamacpp: { endpoint: 'http://localhost:8080' }`
`OPENROUTER_API_KEY` env var populates `openrouter.apiKey` when set
`LLAMACPP_ENDPOINT` env var overrides `llamacpp.endpoint` when set
Env override guard in `config-loader.ts:223` accepts all 4 provider values
Invalid `defaultAssistant` values rejected with loud error at config load time
`KnowledgeConfig` extended with `captureProvider` and `compileProvider` fields, both defaulting to 'claude'
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Modify `packages/core/src/config/config-loader.ts:172-209` (defaults block) and the merge logic. Add env var reads for `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` and `LLAMACPP_ENDPOINT`. Follow existing pattern: `applyEnvOverrides()` at line 223. Add `captureProvider`/`compileProvider` to `KnowledgeConfig` in `config-types.ts` with validation against the 4-provider enum.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 2. Files: config-loader.ts, config-types.ts, config-loader.test.ts

userStories.title: Canonical tool definitions (JSON Schema)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Archon's tool surface defined in OpenAI function-call JSON Schema format so that the tool loop and new providers can consume a single canonical definition.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `packages/core/src/clients/tool-definitions.ts` exports an array of tool definitions
Tools defined: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep, WebFetch, WebSearch
Each definition follows OpenAI function-call format: `{ type: 'function', function: { name, description, parameters: JSONSchema } }`
Parameter schemas match Claude SDK tool surface (file paths, content, regex patterns, etc.)
Exported type `ToolDefinition` is fully typed (no `any`)
Definitions are filterable by name (for `allowed_tools`/`denied_tools`)
Type-check passes
Lint passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Mirror the tool surface from Claude Agent SDK. Parameter schemas should be precise JSON Schema (required fields, string formats, enums where applicable). Export both the definitions array and a lookup map by tool name. Reference Claude SDK tool behavior for parameter semantics.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 3. Files: tool-definitions.ts

userStories.title: Tool implementations: file ops (read, write, edit)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want file operation tools implemented so that the tool loop can read, write, and edit files within worktree-scoped directories.
acceptanceCriteria: New files: `packages/core/src/clients/tools/read.ts`, `write.ts`, `edit.ts`
Read tool: accepts file path + optional offset/limit, returns file content with line numbers
Write tool: accepts file path + content, writes file, returns success confirmation
Edit tool: accepts file path + old_string + new_string, performs exact string replacement
All tools validate that paths are within the provided `cwd` (no traversal above worktree root)
All tools throw on error with descriptive messages (file not found, permission denied, old_string not found)
Each tool exports a function matching signature `(params: Record<string, unknown>, cwd: string) => Promise<string>`
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Use Node.js `fs` APIs. Path validation: resolve the absolute path and verify it starts with the normalized `cwd`. Edit tool must fail if `old_string` is not unique in the file (match Claude SDK behavior). Use `@archon/git`'s `execFileAsync` pattern for consistency.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 4. Files: path-validation.ts, read.ts, write.ts, edit.ts, read.test.ts, write.test.ts, edit.test.ts

userStories.title: Tool implementations: search + shell (glob, grep, bash)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want search and shell tools implemented so that the tool loop can find files, search content, and execute shell commands.
acceptanceCriteria: New files: `packages/core/src/clients/tools/glob.ts`, `grep.ts`, `bash.ts`
Glob tool: accepts pattern + optional path, returns matching file paths sorted by modification time
Grep tool: accepts pattern + optional path/glob/type filters, returns matching lines or file paths
Bash tool: accepts command string + optional timeout, executes via `execFileAsync`, returns stdout/stderr
Bash tool uses `execFileAsync` from `@archon/git` (never `exec`)
All tools scope operations to provided `cwd`
Bash tool enforces timeout (default 120s, max 600s)
Each tool exports a function matching `(params: Record<string, unknown>, cwd: string) => Promise<string>`
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Grep: delegate to ripgrep (`rg`) if available, fall back to Node.js regex search. Glob: use fast-glob or similar. Bash: spawn via `execFileAsync(['bash', '-c', command], { cwd })`. All output should be truncated at a reasonable limit (e.g., 50KB) with a note if truncated.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 5. Files: glob.ts, grep.ts, bash.ts, glob.test.ts, grep.test.ts, bash.test.ts

userStories.title: Tool implementations: web (web-fetch, web-search)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want web tools implemented so that the tool loop can fetch URLs and perform web searches.
acceptanceCriteria: New files: `packages/core/src/clients/tools/web-fetch.ts`, `web-search.ts`
WebFetch tool: accepts URL, returns response body (text or JSON)
WebSearch tool: accepts query string, returns search results
WebFetch respects reasonable timeout (30s default)
WebFetch handles common content types (text/html, application/json, text/plain)
Both tools return errors as structured messages (not thrown exceptions that crash the loop)
Each tool exports a function matching `(params: Record<string, unknown>, cwd: string) => Promise<string>`
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: WebFetch: use native `fetch()`. WebSearch: consider using a search API or returning a 'not available' message if no search API key is configured. Output should be truncated at a reasonable limit. HTML responses should be converted to readable text.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 6. Files: web-fetch.ts, web-search.ts, web-fetch.test.ts, web-search.test.ts

userStories.title: Agentic tool-execution loop core
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a generic agentic tool-execution loop so that stateless providers can execute multi-turn tool-calling conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `packages/core/src/clients/tool-loop.ts`
Loop accepts: messages history, tool definitions, provider endpoint config, streaming callback
Loop implements: send request -> check for tool_calls -> execute tools -> append results -> repeat
Single-turn path (no tool_calls): emits result and exits with zero extra overhead
Tool execution uses the canonical tool implementations from US-004/005/006
Loop yields `MessageChunk` events matching existing discriminated union (`packages/core/src/types/index.ts:195-229`)

Malformed tool-call detection: counter increments on empty/unparseable/schema-violating tool_calls; resets on success; throws classified error after 3 consecutive failures (configurable via `maxMalformedToolCallAttempts`)
Loop respects `abortSignal` for cancellation
Streaming: chunks emitted in real-time as they arrive from the provider
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Core algorithm per PRD section 3. The loop is provider-agnostic — it receives an HTTP endpoint config and message format. Tool dispatch uses a registry built from `tool-definitions.ts` + tool implementations. The loop must handle both streamed and non-streamed responses. Export `ToolLoopConfig` interface for configuration.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 7. Files: packages/core/src/clients/tool-loop.ts, tool-loop.test.ts.

userStories.title: MCP client wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an MCP client that spawns MCP servers and translates their tools to OpenAI function-call format so that non-Claude providers can use MCP tools.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `packages/core/src/clients/mcp-client.ts`
Uses `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` client library to spawn and communicate with MCP servers
Enumerates each server's tools and translates them to OpenAI function-call format
Supports env var expansion in MCP server configs (matching Claude SDK semantics)
Returns merged tool list compatible with `tool-definitions.ts` format
Handles MCP server lifecycle: spawn on demand, clean shutdown on workflow completion
Throws classified errors on MCP server connection failures
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Reference existing MCP handling in `dag-executor.ts:261-294` (`loadMcpConfig()`). The wrapper should accept the same MCP config format used in workflow YAML. Tool parameter schemas from MCP must be translated to JSON Schema function format. Export `McpToolProvider` class or similar.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 8. Files: packages/core/src/clients/mcp-client.ts, packages/core/src/clients/mcp-client.test.ts. Added @modelcontextprotocol/sdk as direct dependency. Used direct JSON-RPC implementation for stdio transport since SDK doesn't export transport classes.

userStories.title: Skill loader
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a skill loader that reads skill definitions and produces system-prompt injections plus scoped tool allowlists so that non-Claude providers can use skills.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `packages/core/src/clients/skill-loader.ts`
Reads skill definitions from directories specified in workflow YAML `skills:` field
Extracts system-prompt content from skill files
Produces scoped tool allowlists based on skill configuration
Returns a `SkillContext` object with `systemPromptAdditions: string[]` and `toolAllowlist: string[]`
Handles missing skill directories with classified errors (no silent skip)
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Reference existing skill handling in `dag-executor.ts:570-594` which wraps nodes in AgentDefinition for Claude. The loader needs to produce equivalent information in a provider-agnostic format. Skill definitions follow Claude's AgentDefinition structure.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 9. Files: packages/core/src/clients/skill-loader.ts, packages/core/src/clients/skill-loader.test.ts.

userStories.title: JSON Schema to GBNF translator
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a JSON Schema to GBNF translator so that Llama.cpp can enforce structured output via its native grammar field.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `packages/core/src/clients/grammar/json-schema-to-gbnf.ts`
Translates JSON Schema to GBNF grammar format
Covers the JSON Schema subset Archon uses: object, string, number, boolean, enum, array of primitives/objects, required, nested objects
Throws loud classified error for unsupported JSON Schema features (no silent degradation)
Pure function with no side effects: `(schema: Record<string, unknown>) => string`
Prefers a maintained library if one exists covering the required subset; otherwise implements minimal translator
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Per PRD section 3b. Llama.cpp uses GBNF via the `grammar` field, not OpenAI's `response_format`. Check for existing `json-schema-to-gbnf` npm packages first. Round-trip tests should verify GBNF constrains output to schema-conforming JSON.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 10. Files: grammar/json-schema-to-gbnf.ts, grammar/json-schema-to-gbnf.test.ts. No maintained npm package found; implemented minimal translator covering required JSON Schema subset.

userStories.title: Context-window manager + summarization
userStories.description: As an operator, I want automatic context-window management so that long-running sessions on stateless providers don't exceed model limits.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `packages/core/src/clients/context-window.ts`
Estimates token count of outgoing request (system + tools + messages)
Uses model's declared tokenizer when known; falls back to conservative character-based estimator
Triggers summarization when estimated tokens exceed `window * reservationThreshold` (default 0.75)
Summarizes oldest contiguous turns, preserving recent turns verbatim
Inserts synthetic summary message in messages table, linking summarized turn IDs
Marks summarized turns as `summarized: true`
Replay reconstructs: system prompt + summary messages + non-summarized verbatim turns
Throws loud classified error on summarization failure (API error, malformed summary, still too large after retry)
Does NOT affect Claude/Codex SDK paths
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Per PRD section 3c. Requires the `kind`/`summarized`/`summary_of` columns from US-012 migration. Summarization calls the same provider with a summarization prompt. Export `ContextWindowManager` class with `estimateTokens()`, `shouldSummarize()`, `summarize()` methods.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 11. Files: packages/core/src/clients/context-window.ts, packages/core/src/clients/context-window.test.ts. ContextWindowManager class with estimateTokens(), shouldSummarize(), summarize() methods. 24 tests. DB persistence deferred to US-012/US-017.

userStories.title: DB migrations (messages columns + token usage table)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want database schema changes so that message history supports stateless-provider replay and token usage is tracked.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration adds `kind VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'text'` to `remote_agent_messages` (values: text, tool_call, tool_result, summary)
New migration adds `summarized BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE` to `remote_agent_messages`
New migration adds `summary_of UUID[] NULL` to `remote_agent_messages`
Backfill: existing rows remain `kind='text'`, `summarized=false`, `summary_of=NULL`
New `remote_agent_token_usage` table with: id, workflow_run_id, conversation_id, node_id, provider, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens, cost_usd, created_at
Foreign keys: workflow_run_id -> workflow_runs (CASCADE), conversation_id -> conversations (CASCADE)
Migration works on both SQLite and PostgreSQL
Type-check passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Follow existing migration pattern in `migrations/` directory. SQLite doesn't support UUID[] — use TEXT with JSON array for SQLite adapter. The `IDatabase` interface abstracts PostgreSQL/SQLite differences. Token usage table per PRD section 5.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 12. Files: 021_messages_columns_token_usage.sql, 000_combined.sql, sqlite.ts, messages.ts, messages.test.ts

userStories.title: OpenAI-compatible base client
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a shared OpenAI-compatible base client so that OpenRouter and Llama.cpp clients share HTTP/streaming/tool-loop logic.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `packages/core/src/clients/openai-compatible.ts`
Implements `IAssistantClient` interface
Accepts configurable endpoint URL, API key, default headers
Builds `ChatCompletionRequest` with messages, tools, response_format
Sends via `fetch()` with SSE streaming support
Parses `ChatCompletionChunk` responses
Yields `MessageChunk` events matching existing discriminated union
Integrates with tool-execution loop from US-007 for tool_calls handling
Integrates with context-window manager from US-011
Supports rate limiting with exponential backoff (matching existing Claude client pattern)
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Per PRD section 2. This is the base that `OpenRouterClient` and `LlamaCppClient` extend/compose. SSE parsing for streaming: handle `data: [DONE]`, `data: {...}` chunks. Map OpenAI response structure to Archon's `MessageChunk` union. The base handles the common HTTP path; subclasses add provider-specific headers and config.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 13. Files: openai-compatible.ts, openai-compatible.test.ts.

userStories.title: OpenRouter client
userStories.description: As an operator, I want an OpenRouter provider client so that workflows can use any model available on OpenRouter.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `packages/core/src/clients/openrouter.ts`
Extends/composes `OpenAICompatibleClient` from US-013
Implements `IAssistantClient` with `getType()` returning 'openrouter'
Sends `HTTP-Referer` header from `siteUrl` config
Sends `X-Title` header from `siteName` config
Uses `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` env var or `apiKey` config for authentication
Model routing via `model` field (e.g., `anthropic/claude-3-haiku`, `meta-llama/llama-4-scout`)
Extracts cost data from OpenRouter response headers when available
Throws classified error when API key is missing
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: OpenRouter endpoint: `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions`. Model IDs use `vendor/model` format. Cost data may be in response headers or body — check OpenRouter API docs. Config from `assistants.openrouter` in `.archon/config.yaml`.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 14. Files: openrouter.ts, openrouter.test.ts

userStories.title: Llama.cpp client
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a Llama.cpp provider client so that workflows can use local models served by llama-server.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `packages/core/src/clients/llamacpp.ts`
Extends/composes `OpenAICompatibleClient` from US-013
Implements `IAssistantClient` with `getType()` returning 'llamacpp'
Uses `LLAMACPP_ENDPOINT` env var or `endpoint` config (default `http://localhost:8080`)
No API key required (local server)
Model loaded server-side; `model` field is informational
Uses GBNF grammar translator from US-010 for `output_format` (passes via `grammar` field, not `response_format`)
Throws classified error when endpoint is unreachable
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Llama.cpp server speaks OpenAI-compatible protocol at `/v1/chat/completions`. Override the `output_format` handling from the base class: instead of `response_format`, translate JSON Schema to GBNF and pass via the `grammar` request field. Config from `assistants.llamacpp` in `.archon/config.yaml`.
userStories.notes: Implemented. Files: packages/core/src/clients/llamacpp.ts, llamacpp.test.ts

userStories.title: Client factory expansion + barrel exports
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the client factory to instantiate OpenRouter and Llama.cpp clients so that the new providers are available throughout the system.
acceptanceCriteria: Factory switch in `packages/core/src/clients/factory.ts` includes `case 'openrouter'` and `case 'llamacpp'`
Error message on `default` case lists all 4 supported providers
Barrel exports in `packages/core/src/clients/index.ts` include new client classes
Factory accepts config for new providers (endpoint, API key)
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Modify `packages/core/src/clients/factory.ts:26-37`. Update the error message in the default case. Import `OpenRouterClient` and `LlamaCppClient`. Consider whether config should be passed to constructors or read from env/config within the factory.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 16. Files: factory.ts, index.ts, factory.test.ts

userStories.title: Message history replay for stateless providers
userStories.description: As an operator, I want message history persisted and replayed for stateless providers so that workflows can resume after failure.
acceptanceCriteria: Extended `addMessage()` in `packages/core/src/db/messages.ts` handles: system, user, assistant (with tool_calls), tool (with tool_call_id), summary messages
Extended `listMessages()` returns messages with `kind`, `summarized`, `summary_of` columns
New `listMessagesForReplay()` function returns non-summarized messages plus ordered summary messages
Replay constructs OpenAI-format `messages[]` array: system prompt -> summaries in position -> verbatim turns
Claude/Codex paths unaffected (they don't use replay)
Tool call metadata round-trips through JSONB correctly
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Per PRD section 4. Extend `MessageRow` interface in `packages/core/src/db/messages.ts:14-21` with new columns. The replay builder should produce an array compatible with OpenAI's chat/completions `messages` parameter. Requires US-012 migration columns.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 17. Files: messages.ts, messages.test.ts

userStories.title: Cost/token tracking persistence layer
userStories.description: As an operator, I want token usage and cost data persisted so that I can track spend across providers and workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `packages/core/src/db/token-usage.ts` with CRUD operations for `remote_agent_token_usage` table
`recordTokenUsage()` inserts a row with workflow_run_id, conversation_id, node_id, provider, model, input/output/total tokens, cost_usd
`getTokenUsageByRun(runId)` returns all usage records for a workflow run
`getTokenUsageSummary(filters)` returns aggregated usage by provider/model/time range
New `recordTokenUsage()` method added to `IWorkflowStore` interface in `packages/workflows/src/store.ts`
Implemented in `packages/core/src/workflows/store-adapter.ts`
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Per PRD section 5. Follow existing DB module patterns in `packages/core/src/db/`. Use `IDatabase` interface for PostgreSQL/SQLite compatibility. Cost may be null for providers that don't report cost. Token counts from `MessageChunk.tokens` and `MessageChunk.cost` fields.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 18. Files: token-usage.ts, token-usage.test.ts, store.ts, store-adapter.ts, package.json

userStories.title: Tool loop feature integration (allowed_tools, denied_tools, output_format, systemPrompt)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want allowed_tools, denied_tools, output_format, and systemPrompt to work on all providers so that feature parity is complete for these axes.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool loop filters tool definitions by `allowed_tools` before sending to API
Tool loop excludes `denied_tools` from tool list before sending
Tool loop prepends `systemPrompt` as first system message in messages array
Tool loop maps `output_format` to `response_format: { type: 'json_schema', json_schema: ... }` for OpenRouter
Tool loop maps `output_format` to GBNF grammar for Llama.cpp (via US-010 translator)
All four features work identically across all providers that use the tool loop
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Modify `packages/core/src/clients/tool-loop.ts`. `allowed_tools`: if set, include only named tools from canonical + MCP + skill sets. `denied_tools`: exclude named tools. These filter BEFORE sending the request. `systemPrompt`: insert as `{ role: 'system', content: systemPrompt }` at position 0. `output_format`: provider-specific mapping.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 19. Files: tool-loop.ts, tool-loop.test.ts, openai-compatible.ts, openai-compatible.test.ts, llamacpp.ts

userStories.title: Tool loop hooks lifecycle
userStories.description: As an operator, I want hooks to fire in the tool loop so that hook-based workflows work on all providers.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool loop fires `PreToolUse` hook before each tool execution
Tool loop fires `PostToolUse` hook after each successful tool execution
Tool loop fires `PostToolUseFailure` hook after failed tool execution
Hook callbacks receive the same payload shape as Claude SDK hooks
Hooks can modify tool call behavior (e.g., deny a tool call)
Hook errors are surfaced, not swallowed
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Reference existing hook implementation in `dag-executor.ts:236-253` (`buildSDKHooksFromYAML()`) and Claude client hooks at `claude.ts:347-404`. The tool loop must fire hooks at equivalent lifecycle points. Use the same hook payload interface. Export `ToolLoopHooks` type.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 20. Files: tool-loop.ts, tool-loop.test.ts

userStories.title: Tool loop MCP + skills integration
userStories.description: As an operator, I want MCP tools and skills injected into the tool loop so that all providers have access to MCP servers and skill-scoped behaviors.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool loop accepts MCP tool definitions from US-008 MCP client and merges them into the tool list
MCP tool calls in `tool_calls` are dispatched to the MCP client for execution
MCP tool results are formatted and appended to message history
Tool loop accepts skill context from US-009 and injects system-prompt additions
Skill tool allowlists are intersected with `allowed_tools` if both are specified
MCP server lifecycle is managed: spawned before loop, cleaned up after
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Wire `McpToolProvider` from US-008 and `SkillContext` from US-009 into tool-loop.ts. MCP tools should be indistinguishable from canonical tools in the tool list. Skills add to the system prompt and may restrict the tool set. Env var expansion in MCP configs happens at loop invocation time.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 21. Files: tool-loop.ts, tool-loop.test.ts, openai-compatible.ts, types/index.ts

userStories.title: Codex feature parity (SDK-native + tool-loop fallback)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want Codex to support all features Claude supports so that provider choice doesn't limit available features.
acceptanceCriteria: Research completed: documented which features `@openai/codex-sdk` supports natively vs. requires tool-loop fallback
Features the Codex SDK supports natively are wired up directly (remove 'Claude-only' warning branches)
Features the Codex SDK does NOT support are routed through Archon's tool loop (calling OpenAI API directly with Codex model ID)
`CodexClient.sendQuery()` dispatches: SDK path when all features supported, tool-loop path otherwise
Dispatch is transparent to callers (`getType()` still returns 'codex')
Workflow event emitted tagging whether a Codex node ran SDK or tool-loop path
Existing Codex workflows without unsupported features run unchanged on SDK path
Type-check passes
Tests pass
userStories.technicalNotes: Per PRD section 7. Read the installed `@openai/codex-sdk` package to determine native support for: `allowed_tools`, `denied_tools`, `hooks`, `mcp`, `skills`, `output_format`, `systemPrompt`, `fallbackModel`, `betas`, `sandbox`. Modify `packages/core/src/clients/codex.ts`. The dispatch logic inspects `options` to decide which path to take.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 22. Files: codex.ts, codex.test.ts, package.json. SDK supports: model, modelReasoningEffort, webSearchMode, additionalDirectories, outputFormat, abortSignal. Tool-loop fallback for: allowed_tools, denied_tools, hooks, mcp, skills, systemPrompt, effort, thinking, maxBudgetUsd, fallbackModel, betas, sandbox.

userStories.title: DAG executor new provider branching + validator cleanup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the DAG executor to handle openrouter and llamacpp providers so that workflow nodes can target new providers.
acceptanceCriteria: `resolveNodeProviderAndModel()` in `dag-executor.ts` adds branches for 'openrouter' and 'llamacpp'
Option assembly for new providers passes all features through to the tool loop (no silent skips)
`buildLoopNodeOptions()` handles new provider branches
Validator in `packages/workflows/src/validator.ts` removes Claude-only warnings for features now supported on all providers
Validator adds new provider-specific validations (e.g., OpenRouter requires API key, Llama.cpp requires endpoint)
